concepts.title: Time Period of SHM
concepts.conceptBody:
Time Period of SHM

Periodic functions $\text{f}\left( \text{t} \right)$ with period $\text{T}$ are those functions of the variable ' $\text{t}$ ' which have the property,

$\text{f}\left( \text{t}+\text{T} \right)=\text{f}\left( \text{t} \right)$

Both $\text{sin}\left( \omega t+\phi \right)$ and $\text{cos}\left( \omega t+\phi \right)$ will repeat their values if the angle $\left( \omega t+\phi \right)$ increases by $2\pi $ or its multiple. As $\text{T}$ is the $2\pi $ smallest time for repetition.

\(\begin{aligned} \omega(\mathrm{t}+\mathrm{T})+\phi & =\omega \mathrm{t}+\phi+2 \pi \\ \therefore \omega \mathrm{T} & =2 \pi \text { or } \mathrm{T}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~T}}{\omega}\end{aligned}\)

Since

$\begin{matrix} \omega =\sqrt{\frac{\text{k}}{\text{m}}} \\ \text{T}=2\pi \sqrt{\frac{\text{m}}{\text{k}}} \\ \end{matrix}$

concepts.title: Frequency of SHM
concepts.conceptBody:
Frequency And Angular Frequency

Frequency is defined as the number of oscillations per second or simply the reciprocal of time period.

$\begin{matrix} v=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ T}}=\frac{\omega }{2\pi }=\frac{1}{2\pi }\sqrt{\frac{\text{k}}{\text{m}}} \\ v=\frac{1}{2\pi }\sqrt{\frac{\text{k}}{\text{m}}} \\ \end{matrix}$

The constant $\omega $ is called the angular frequency. The angular frequency and period in simple harmonic motion are independent of the amplitude.
